candidate_summary: The bona fide purchaser (BFP) doctrine: an innocent purchaser for value who takes property without notice (actual, constructive, or imputed) of prior adverse claims takes clean title, defeating prior equitable interests of which they did not have notice. The doctrine has a duty-of-inquiry requirement (BFP must make 'reasonable inspections') and protections against good-faith third-party transactions.
